title: Challenge Lab - Amazon Elastic Container Service using Fargate

steps.text: Open the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab via Open Console.
Retain the default Account ID, enter the provided IAM Username and Password, and sign in.
Select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.

XYZ Company wants to deploy a simple containerized web application using Amazon ECS with AWS Fargate to avoid managing infrastructure. Your job is to set up an ECS cluster, define a container and task, create a service, test the deployment, and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Navigate to Elastic Container Service via the Services menu under Containers with fargate type.
Go to Task Definitions and Create new task definition twice.
Configure task definition: CPU: Choose .25 vCPU. Memory: Choose 1 GB.
Copy the ip from networking and paste it in browser.
Confirm the cluster , task definition , and service are running.
Verify the web app shows "It works" at the public IP.
Click Validate in the lab console to check the setup.
Sign out of AWS by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
Click End Lab in the lab console to complete the lab.
